deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding rather than giving answers—i.e., it never articulates a rationale such as 'I'm asking you to work this out because active reasoning builds retention' or similar. The tutoring constraints are enforced but the pedagogical rationale is never stated.

In all three transcripts, when students make meaningful improvements or arrive at correct reasoning (e.g., T1 Turn 6-7 on boundary consistency, T2 Turn 10 on city-proper vs. metro, T3 Turn 5 on consistency principle), the tutor moves forward without prompting the student to reflect on what changed in their thinking or why the improved approach worked.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts, the tutor demonstrates strong Socratic practice: it never gives direct answers, consistently asks students to reason before confirming, builds on student progress, and keeps responses concise and well-targeted. The main weaknesses are the absence of any explicit rationale for the tutoring approach (1.3.A.a) and a consistent failure to prompt reflection after students improve (1.3.C.a). No other rubric criteria show clear evidence of deduction.
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1: The tutor never provides near-submission-ready output, never rewrites student text, and never states conclusions before the student reasons toward them. Full marks. 1.2: The tutor consistently acknowledges correct student steps and builds on them; it uses diagnostic questions before addressing confusion. Full marks. 1.3: The tutor never explains why it is guiding rather than answering (1.3.A.a, -2), and in all three transcripts it moves on after student improvement without prompting reflection on what changed (1.3.C.a, -2). 1.3.B is borderline—the tutor does give process-level feedback (e.g., 'consistency matters more than chasing the biggest number'), so no deduction there. Dialogue quality and communication quality are both strong: no repetition without progress, no off-topic drift, responses are bite-sized, and tone is consistently supportive and formative.
